senses.gloss: to go on foot, to walk
an extremity; a limb; a leg
an extremity; a limb; a hind leg
neither lank nor crisp, neither scrawny nor shredded, neither svelte nor portly, well on foot due to not having excess mass

examples.text: رَأَيْتُ مُوسَى وَإِذَا رَجُلٌ ضَرْبٌ رَجِلٌ، كَأَنَّهُ مِنْ رِجَالِ شَنُوءَةَ، وَرَأَيْتُ عِيسَى، فَإِذَا هُوَ رَجُلٌ رَبْعَةٌ أَحْمَرُ كَأَنَّمَا خَرَجَ مِنْ دِيمَاسٍ
examples.english: I saw Moses who was a man neither of a scrawny nor a shredded stature, as though he were of the Šanūʾa men, and I saw Jesus, cobby and red as though he had come out of a bathhouse.

senses.gloss: foal which is allowed to suck freely

etymology.text:
Originally "walker", attested with the meaning "footsoldier" as Safaitic 𐪇𐪔𐪁 (rgl).

From Proto-Semitic *rigl-.
